content: First-line treatments include SSRIs (escitalopram, sertraline, paroxetine) and SNRIs (venlafaxine, duloxetine). Buspirone is an alternative anxiolytic. Benzodiazepines provide rapid relief but are limited to short-term use due to dependence risk. Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) is equally effective as medication and may have more durable benefits after discontinuation; key components include cognitive restructuring, worry exposure, and relaxation training. Combination therapy (medication + CBT) may benefit severe cases. Treatment duration typically 6-12 months after symptom remission before gradual tapering. Pregabalin shows efficacy in some studies.
